How can we fight the slop-laden term “agentic” AI before it’s too late?

I fear that it really is about to stick - and historically when that happens you can’t fix it later.

What is your favorite alternative? Or do we just accept it and move on?

NEW paper from Google DeepMind

The biggest threat to AI agents isn't a smarter attacker. It's the web itself.

This work introduces the first systematic framework for understanding how the open web can be weaponized against autonomous agents.

- all methods from 15-72% use dense retrieval models or BM25
- all methods above 80-91% use late interaction models, from LightOn and Mixedbread

I try not to get cranky about (wrong) AI-generated math slop, but it really is harmful. Our tools for verifying correctness are time-intensive; the  refereeing system relies on good faith of authors. Tempting to take a heads-on-pikes approach to people who take advantage.

I built a very complex bio/chem app for macOS with Codex. It's still alpha, but it's crazy what I have now! It took 75 prompts & about half of Codex's 5.4 xHigh weekly rate. I tried the same prompts with Claude code & ran out of weekly limits before completing 25% of the prompts!
